observation: K505 builds the per-agent substrate-savings telemetry pipeline: Bishop (run_session_end extension), Knight (knight_session_savings.py), Pawn (pawn_session_savings.py). All three write to a shared substrate_savings_log.jsonl via append-record pattern.

Cold multipliers are evidence-informed conservative estimates from R13 empirical baseline: Bishop 3.0× (flagship-tier, within-Anthropic 21.6× R13), Knight 2.5× (Sonnet with substrate ≈ Opus cold per B123), Pawn 3.5× (includes friction multiplier: 3+ confirmations before action). All marked multiplier_provisional: true until K-future calibration.

Substrate overhead tokens (Librarian MCP calls, memory reads, Cathedral lookups) are subtracted from savings BEFORE reporting net_savings. overhead_cost = (substrate_overhead_tokens / 1M) × vendor_input_rate. This is the cost of the substrate itself — not ignored per #41 good-name guardrail.

Pawn cold_multiplier = 3.5× bakes in friction: cold Pawn without substrate requires 3+ yes/that/do-it confirmation rounds before action. Each round ≈ 1 full re-prompt of average input tokens. friction_confirmations field per Pawn task enables calibration of actual average over time.

substrate_savings_log.jsonl schema: ts, agent, session_id, input_tokens, output_tokens, substrate_overhead_tokens, substrate_injection_count, vendor, model, actual_cost_usd, counterfactual_cost_usd, session_savings_usd, cold_multiplier, friction_confirmations, multiplier_provisional, notes. Append-only, JSONL format.

Two new MCP tools added to librarian-mcp server.ts: record_substrate_savings (appends one session's savings record, returns formatted summary) and substrate_savings_summary (reads log, returns aggregate by window=all|7d|30d and agent filter). run_session_end extended with optional token count params for auto-logging.

run_session_end MCP tool extended in K505 with optional params: input_tokens, output_tokens, substrate_overhead_tokens, substrate_injection_count, vendor, model, friction_confirmations. When token counts are supplied, auto-logs substrate savings and appends '── Substrate Savings This Session ──' to the session-end output.

FounderSavingsDashboard.tsx at /founder-savings (DSS portal, tools group, ProtectedRoute). Reads /founder-savings-data.json (static snapshot generated by generate-savings-snapshot.mjs). Shows all-time/7d/30d windows, per-agent cards (Bishop violet, Knight blue, Pawn amber), savings ratio headline, provisional flag, calibration note.

generate-savings-snapshot.mjs reads substrate_savings_log.jsonl and writes platform/public/founder-savings-data.json with all three time windows pre-aggregated. Invoked via npm run generate:savings-snapshot. Dashboard fetches this static file at runtime — works both locally and on deployed the2ndsecond.com.

K505 vendor pricing table (per 1M tokens, USD): anthropic $3.00/$15.00, openai $2.50/$10.00, google $1.25/$5.00, perplexity $1.00/$1.00. Applied to compute actual_cost_usd = (input_tokens/1M × input_rate) + (output_tokens/1M × output_rate).

K505_CALIBRATION_PLAN.md documents 30-day recurring calibration procedure: compute empirical_multiplier from 30-day window data, compare to provisional, update constants in server.ts/scripts if >20% drift, log calibration event as Scribe R11 entry. Pawn friction calibrated separately from friction_confirmations field.

K505 + R13 together close the empirical gap: R13 measures Cathedral Effect at population level (8 models × 50 questions, +86.2pp lift, 21.6× cost reduction). K505 measures at personal level (every B/K/P session, actual cost vs honest counterfactual). Combined claim: same architectural property measured at academic-research scale AND single-user-personal scale.

K505 Phase D.2 hooks into K502 LB Test Frame member dashboard — when K502 lands, members see their personal substrate savings from running LB Frame AI sessions. Same math, same honest counterfactuals. Aggregate sharing requires explicit opt-in per BRIDLE privacy guardrail. Founder dashboard is LB-internal version of what every member gets.

knight_session_savings.py report command outputs a pre-formatted markdown table for paste into REPORT_KNIGHT_K###.md Substrate Savings subsection: session_id, model/vendor, token counts, overhead, actual/counterfactual/net savings, provisional flag. Bishop K-session reports can auto-populate this section.
